code: @Manual{,   title = {astrocoords: Astronomical Coordinates for R},   author = {Grigory Uskov},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1},   url = {https://uskovgs.github.io/astrocoords/}, }

title: Astronomical Coordinates for R
text: astrocoords R package basic astronomical coordinate time conversion. Core coordinate time calculations astrocoords based ERFA library. currently supports: ICRS Galactic sky coordinates angular separation sphere simple frame transformation ICRS Galactic Julian Date / Modified Julian Date conversion

title: Astronomical Coordinates for R
text: can install development version astrocoords GitHub :

title: Astronomical Coordinates for R
text: Create coordinates ICRS frame: Transform Galactic coordinates: Measure angular separation arcseconds: Parse compact coordinate string: Work Julian Date:
code: library(astrocoords) #>  #> Attaching package: 'astrocoords' #> The following object is masked from 'package:graphics': #>  #>     frame  x <- ra_dec(c(10, 20), c(30, 40)) x #> <sky_coord[2] icrs> #> [1] 00h40m00.0s +30°00'00" 01h19m60.0s +40°00'00" transform_to(x, galactic()) #> <sky_coord[2] galactic> #> [1] 119°59'08.0" -32°48'23" 128°50'58.6" -22°32'36" separation(ra_dec(10, 20), ra_dec(11, 21)) #> [1] 4932.552 parse_coord("J230631.0+155633") #> <sky_coord[1] icrs> #> [1] 23h06m31.0s +15°56'33" jd <- datetime_to_jd(as.POSIXct("2026-03-08 12:34:56", tz = "UTC")) jd #> [1] 2461108  jd_to_datetime(jd, tz = "UTC") #> [1] "2026-03-08 12:34:56 UTC"

text: x, y `<sky_coord>` vectors. max_sep Maximum separation threshold. unit Separation unit: `"arcsec"`, `"arcmin"`, `"deg"`, `"rad"`. method Matching backend: `"kdtree"` `"bruteforce"`.

title: Match two sky catalogs within angular radius — coord_match
Match two sky catalogs within angular radius — coord_match
text: frames differ, `y` transformed frame `x`. Rows sorted `x_id`, `sep`, `y_id`. `x_id` matches, one row returned `y_id = NA` `sep = NA`.

what: Convert date-time to Julian Date (JD) — datetime_to_jd
title: Convert date-time to Julian Date (JD) — datetime_to_jd
text: Convert regular calendar date-times Julian Date.

title: Convert date-time to Julian Date (JD) — datetime_to_jd
Convert date-time to Julian Date (JD) — datetime_to_jd
text: x Date-time values `POSIXct`, `POSIXlt`, `Date`. `Date` values treated midnight (`00:00:00`) UTC.

title: Convert date-time to Julian Date (JD) — datetime_to_jd
Convert date-time to Julian Date (JD) — datetime_to_jd
text: JD continuous day count used astronomy. fractional part time day. JD starts noon, midnight shown `.5`. Uses ERFA `scale = "UTC"`. Leap seconds handled ERFA.

title: Convert date-time to Julian Date (JD) — datetime_to_jd
code: old_accuracy <- getOption("digits") options(digits = 11)  t <- as.POSIXct("2026-03-08 12:34:56", tz = "UTC") datetime_to_jd(t) #> [1] 2461108.0243  datetime_to_jd(as.Date("2026-03-08")) #> [1] 2461107.5  options(digits = old_accuracy)

title: Build IAU-style source names from sky coordinates — iau_name
code: iau_name(x, prefix = NULL, epoch = "J", ra_digits = 1, dec_digits = 0)

title: Build IAU-style source names from sky coordinates — iau_name
text: x <sky_coord> vector. prefix Optional string added epoch code. epoch Epoch code, usually "J". ra_digits Number digits decimal point RA seconds. dec_digits Number digits decimal point Dec seconds.

title: Build IAU-style source names from sky coordinates — iau_name
Build IAU-style source names from sky coordinates — iau_name
code: x <- parse_coord("J230631.0+155633")  iau_name(x) #> [1] "J230631.0+155633" iau_name(x, prefix = "SRGA J") #> [1] "SRGA J230631.0+155633" iau_name(x, ra_digits = 2, dec_digits = 1) #> [1] "J230631.00+155633.0"  # Non-ICRS inputs are transformed internally iau_name(transform_to(x, galactic())) #> [1] "J230631.0+155633"

title: Convert Julian Date (JD) to date-time — jd_to_datetime

text: jd Numeric vector Julian Date values. tz Time zone output `POSIXct` values.

title: Convert Julian Date (JD) to date-time — jd_to_datetime
Convert Julian Date (JD) to date-time — jd_to_datetime
text: `tz` changes time displayed, physical moment. Uses ERFA `scale = "UTC"`. Leap seconds handled ERFA.

title: Convert Julian Date (JD) to date-time — jd_to_datetime
code: jd <- 2461107.5 jd_to_datetime(jd, tz = "UTC") #> [1] "2026-03-08 UTC" jd_to_datetime(jd, tz = "Europe/Moscow") #> [1] "2026-03-08 MSK"  x <- as.POSIXct("2026-03-08 00:00:00", tz = "UTC") jd_to_datetime(datetime_to_jd(x), tz = "UTC") #> [1] "2026-03-08 UTC"

what: Set printing style for sky coordinates — set_print_hms
title: Set printing style for sky coordinates — set_print_hms
text: helpers control `sky_coord` objects displayed printed formatted.

title: Set printing style for sky coordinates — set_print_hms
Set printing style for sky coordinates — set_print_hms
Set printing style for sky coordinates — set_print_hms
text: `set_print_hms()` uses sexagesimal notation `00h40m00.0s +20d00'00"`. `set_print_plain()` uses compact plain-text style `00 40 000.0 +20 00 00`. `set_print_pair()` prints decimal degree pairs `(10, 20)`. current style stored `options(astrocoords.notation = ...)`.

title: Set printing style for sky coordinates — set_print_hms
code: x <- ra_dec(10, 20)  set_print_hms() format(x) #> [1] "00h40m00.0s +20°00'00\""  set_print_plain() format(x) #> [1] "00 40 000.0 +20 00 00"  set_print_pair() format(x) #> [1] "(10, 20)"

title: vctrs ptype methods for sky_coord — sky_coord-vctrs-methods
Create sky coordinates — sky_coord
Create sky coordinates — sky_coord
code: sky_coord(lon = double(), lat = double(), frame = icrs())

title: Create sky coordinates — sky_coord
text: lon Longitude-like coordinate degrees. `frame = icrs()`, right ascension (`ra`) must   `0` `360`. `frame = galactic()`, Galactic longitude (`l`) must   `0` `360`. lat Latitude-like coordinate degrees. `frame = icrs()`, declination (`dec`) must   `-90` `90`. `frame = galactic()`, Galactic latitude (`b`) must   `-90` `90`. frame sky frame object. Supported frames icrs() galactic(). default icrs().

title: Create sky coordinates — sky_coord
Create sky coordinates — sky_coord
code: x <- sky_coord(10, 20) x #> <sky_coord[1] icrs> #> [1] (10, 20)  g <- sky_coord(120, -30, frame = galactic()) g #> <sky_coord[1] galactic> #> [1] (120, -30)  ra_dec(10, 20) #> <sky_coord[1] icrs> #> [1] (10, 20) gal_coord(120, -30) #> <sky_coord[1] galactic> #> [1] (120, -30)

what: Transform sky coordinates to another frame — transform_to
title: Transform sky coordinates to another frame — transform_to
text: Convert sky_coord vector supported coordinate frames.

title: Transform sky coordinates to another frame — transform_to
Transform sky coordinates to another frame — transform_to
text: x <sky_coord> vector. frame Target frame. Use icrs() galactic().

title: Transform sky coordinates to another frame — transform_to
Transform sky coordinates to another frame — transform_to
text: Supported transformations : icrs() -> galactic() galactic() -> icrs() x already requested frame, returned unchanged. Use transform_to() (transform()) sky_coord objects.

title: Transform sky coordinates to another frame — transform_to
code: x <- ra_dec(c(10, 120), c(20, -30)) x #> <sky_coord[2] icrs> #> [1] (10, 20)   (120, -30)  g <- transform_to(x, galactic()) g #> <sky_coord[2] galactic> #> [1] (119.2694, -42.79039515) (247.0829, -0.04805424)   transform_to(g, icrs()) #> <sky_coord[2] icrs> #> [1] (10, 20)   (120, -30)  # Pipe-friendly workflow ra_dec(10, 20) |>   transform_to(galactic()) |>   transform_to(icrs()) #> <sky_coord[1] icrs> #> [1] (10, 20)
